RICHARD LAWLER 7:54 AM GMT+8 Leaked video shows Microsoft’s work on a lightweight ‘Copilot OS’ built for AI. Zac Bowden at Windows Central posted this video of Aion, a stripped-down Windows concept made for agentic AI that popped up on the BetaWiki Discord, and says sources have confirmed to him that it is a real video made in 2024. There’s no indication whether an OS like this would actually ship or if it’s part of something like Project Solara, but it’s very Chrome OS-like, built around the Edge browser and web a

Zac Bowden at Windows Central posted this video of Aion, a stripped-down Windows concept made for agentic AI that popped up on the BetaWiki Discord, and says sources have confirmed to him that it is a real video made in 2024.
There’s no indication whether an OS like this would actually ship or if it’s part of something like Project Solara, but it’s very Chrome OS-like, built around the Edge browser and web apps.

TERRENCE O'BRIEN 4:43 AM GMT+8 Support for a social media ban for kids under 16 cuts across party lines. According to a Pew Research poll, 56 percent of American adults support banning social media for anyone under 16. A majority support it across almost every demographic — Democrats, Republicans, parents, non-parents — except among those over 65, where 49 percent of respondents agreed, but just 19 percent opposed a ban. Image: Pew Research Center 19

ELIZABETH LOPATTO 4:38 AM GMT+8 “The CEOs don’t like the world they’ve helped create.” Elon Musk and Joe Lonsdale are doing their best to make sure “the high-tech future will have to be built in a way that blends church, state and market, to the benefit of those most able to seize public resources and turn them to private profit.” The extractive world of today’s technology — harvesting your data and attention, among other things — has created the Texan Ideology. EMMA ROTH 12:29 AM GMT+8 Cloudflare is cracking down on “multi-purpose” crawlers. Starting September 15th, Cloudflare will block bots that scrape ad-supported websites for search indexing and AI training at the same time. The goal is to encourage AI companies to separate their crawlers for different purposes, giving publishers more control over which ones to allow into their websites. Cloudflare's new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers' content | TechCrunch [TECHCRUNCH] 5

Starting September 15th, Cloudflare will block bots that scrape ad-supported websites for search indexing and AI training at the same time. The goal is to encourage AI companies to separate their crawlers for different purposes, giving publishers more control over which ones to allow into their websites.

EMMA ROTH JUL 2 The TV Time app is shutting down after 15 years. The app, which allows you to track the shows and movies you’re watching, will no longer be available starting July 15th. In an update on its support page, TV Time’s developers say operating the free app “was no longer sustainable,” and that “there was not enough demand for a paid app.” Popular Show Tracking App TV Time Shutting Down on July 15 [MACRUMORS] 5

The app, which allows you to track the shows and movies you’re watching, will no longer be available starting July 15th. In an update on its support page, TV Time’s developers say operating the free app “was no longer sustainable,” and that “there was not enough demand for a paid app.”

EMMA ROTH JUL 2 Another alleged Scattered Spider member has been arrested. 19-year-old Peter Stokes was arrested in Finland and extradited to the US to face federal conspiracy charges related to the Scattered Spider hacking group. Stokes is accused of working with co-conspirators to breach a luxury jewelry retailer and demand an $8 million ransom. Last month, two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty to breaching London’s transportation system in 2024. Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland [BBC] 0

JENNIFER PATTISON TUOHY JUL 2 You can now pre-order this $8,000 laundry-folding robot. Made by Weave Robotics, Issac 1 is a fuzzy, colorful home robot that promises to collect dirty clothes, fold and put away laundry, make beds, and tidy clutter. Shipping is slated for later this year, with a $250 deposit required. While its main tasks are automated, Weave says that, like its predecessor Isaac 0, Issac 1 will be teleoperated “when needed to guarantee we complete tasks.” This content isn't visible due to your cookie

Made by Weave Robotics, Issac 1 is a fuzzy, colorful home robot that promises to collect dirty clothes, fold and put away laundry, make beds, and tidy clutter. Shipping is slated for later this year, with a $250 deposit required. While its main tasks are automated, Weave says that, like its predecessor Isaac 0, Issac 1 will be teleoperated “when needed to guarantee we complete tasks.”

Instead of turning M5Stack’s M5PaperS3 development hardware into another Kindle clone, Wenting Zhang used the Playdate’s open source CrankBoy emulator to create a Game Boy alternative with an easy-on-the-eyes E Ink screen. Having to play with touchscreen controls isn’t ideal, but we’d love to see this turned into an app optimized for the Boox Palma devices. This content isn't visible due to your cookie preferences. Instead of turning M5Stack’s M5PaperS3 development hardware into another Kindle clone, Wenting Zhang used the Playdate’s open source CrankBoy emulator to create a Game Boy alternative with an easy-on-the-eyes E Ink screen. Having to play with touchscreen controls isn’t ideal, but we’d love to see this turned into an app optimized for the Boox Palma devices.

JESS WEATHERBED JUL 2 Google’s $4.7 billion Android fine has been upheld. The search giant has lost its final appeal to overturn a record-breaking €4.1 billion (about $4.68 billion) penalty imposed by the European Commission over practices that abused Android’s mobile dominance. The charges date back to 2018, so it’s taken almost a decade to close the antitrust case. DOMINIC PRESTON JUL 2 Honor’s latest foldable is finally out in the UK and Europe. The Magic V6 is available to order now in the UK, Germany, France, Spain, and Italy. ICE has denied it maintains such a database, but Mother Jones lays out some shifty wording that suggests Trump administration officials are dodging the question — including a spontaneous mention of the known Terrorist Screening Dataset. EMMA ROTH JUL 2 Apple’s email-hiding feature is reportedly exposing real email addresses. Tyler Murphy, the co-founder of EasyOptOuts, tells 404 Media that he notified Apple about a bug in its Hide My Email feature more than a year ago, but it’s reportedly still not fixed. The vulnerability allows someone to obtain a person’s real email through the fake one generated by Apple’s tool, 404 Media reports. JESS WEATHERBED JUL 1 Former Sony gaming chief is baffled by the PlayStation PC pullback. In an interview with French media outlet PSI, former SIE Worldwide Studios chairman Shawn Layden said bringing Sony exclusives to PC was always about expanding title visability beyond PlayStation consoles, and wasn’t a money-making strategy: I think if someone’s waiting 18 months for something to come on PC, we didn’t lose a sale to them. They weren’t going to buy the hardware anyway. If it’s a way to cover costs or the burden

In an interview with French media outlet PSI, former SIE Worldwide Studios chairman Shawn Layden said bringing Sony exclusives to PC was always about expanding title visability beyond PlayStation consoles, and wasn’t a money-making strategy:

I think if someone’s waiting 18 months for something to come on PC, we didn’t lose a sale to them. They weren’t going to buy the hardware anyway. If it’s a way to cover costs or the burden of making a port, there is some money attached to that, or the distraction or whatever. I don’t know what they’re thinking.”

There are video versions too, all along similar lines teasing a “new shape” from the company. Image: Samsung 2

I’m planning to write more about this soon, but in the meantime, you should also check out my big story about this case from earlier this year. Answer and Affirmative Defenses of Jon Prosser [DOCUMENTCLOUD] 1 RICHARD LAWLER 8:11 AM GMT+8 iFixit goes to the source to explain how iPhone batteries are made. Instead of revealing more about your iPhones and other devices by tearing them apart, this time iFixit is inside a Chinese battery factory. The video shows how the lithium polymer cells are manufactured and tested, long before they end up in your pocket. If you buy something from a Verge link, Vox Media may earn a commission. Allow View and Manage all cookie consent preferences 0 RICHARD LAWLER 7:54 AM GMT+8 Leaked video shows Microsoft’s work on a lightweight ‘Copilot OS’ built for AI. Zac Bowden at Windows Central posted this video of Aion, a stripped-down Windows concept made for agentic AI that popped up on the BetaWiki Discord, and says sources have confirmed to him that it is a real video made in 2024. There’s no indication whether an OS like this would actually ship or if it’s part of something like Project Solara, but it’s very Chrome OS-like, built around the Edge browser and web apps. Allow View and Manage all cookie consent preferences 3 SEAN HOLLISTER 6:38 AM GMT+8 Microsoft’s answer to the end of PlayStation discs: a bizarre GitHub stunt. You know what weeping gamers really want? A burned CD with their GitHub repo on it. Fun novelty if you can get it, though. “This is a limited run, so signing up doesn’t guarantee one,” writes Microsoft-owned GitHub. This content isn't visible due to your cookie preferences. To load this content, click the Allow button below to opt in to "Social Media & Embedded Content" cookies. These cookies are set and controlled by the third party sources from which the embedded content originates. Allow View and Manage all cookie consent preferences 4 TERRENCE O'BRIEN 4:43 AM GMT+8 Support for a social media ban for kids under 16 cuts across party lines. According to a Pew Research poll, 56 percent of American adults support banning social media for anyone under 16. A majority support it across almost every demographic — Democrats, Republicans, parents, non-parents — except among those over 65, where 49 percent of respondents agreed, but just 19 percent opposed a ban. Image: Pew Research Center 19 ELIZABETH LOPATTO 4:38 AM GMT+8 “The CEOs don’t like the world they’ve helped create.” Elon Musk and Joe Lonsdale are doing their best to make sure “the high-tech future will have to be built in a way that blends church, state and market, to the benefit of those most able to seize public resources and turn them to private profit.” The extractive world of today’s technology — harvesting your data and attention, among other things — has created the Texan Ideology. The Texan Ideology [THE BAFFLER] 6 EMMA ROTH 12:29 AM GMT+8 Cloudflare is cracking down on “multi-purpose” crawlers. Starting September 15th, Cloudflare will block bots that scrape ad-supported websites for search indexing and AI training at the same time. The goal is to encourage AI companies to separate their crawlers for different purposes, giving publishers more control over which ones to allow into their websites. Cloudflare's new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers' content | TechCrunch [TECHCRUNCH] 5 EMMA ROTH JUL 2 The TV Time app is shutting down after 15 years. The app, which allows you to track the shows and movies you’re watching, will no longer be available starting July 15th. In an update on its support page, TV Time’s developers say operating the free app “was no longer sustainable,” and that “there was not enough demand for a paid app.” Popular Show Tracking App TV Time Shutting Down on July 15 [MACRUMORS] 5 Influencer screenings aren’t going away CHARLES PULLIAM-MOOREJUL 29 EMMA ROTH JUL 2 Another alleged Scattered Spider member has been arrested. 19-year-old Peter Stokes was arrested in Finland and extradited to the US to face federal conspiracy charges related to the Scattered Spider hacking group. Stokes is accused of working with co-conspirators to breach a luxury jewelry retailer and demand an $8 million ransom. Last month, two Scattered Spider members pleaded guilty to breaching London’s transportation system in 2024. Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland [BBC] 0 JENNIFER PATTISON TUOHY JUL 2 You can now pre-order this $8,000 laundry-folding robot. Made by Weave Robotics, Issac 1 is a fuzzy, colorful home robot that promises to collect dirty clothes, fold and put away laundry, make beds, and tidy clutter. Shipping is slated for later this year, with a $250 deposit required. While its main tasks are automated, Weave says that, like its predecessor Isaac 0, Issac 1 will be teleoperated “when needed to guarantee we complete tasks.” This content isn't visible due to your cookie preferences. NILAY PATELJUL 22 ANDREW LISZEWSKI JUL 2 The Paper Boy S3 is a Game Boy clone with an E Ink touchscreen. Instead of turning M5Stack’s M5PaperS3 development hardware into another Kindle clone, Wenting Zhang used the Playdate’s open source CrankBoy emulator to create a Game Boy alternative with an easy-on-the-eyes E Ink screen. Having to play with touchscreen controls isn’t ideal, but we’d love to see this turned into an app optimized for the Boox Palma devices. This content isn't visible due to your cookie preferences. YouTuber Urban Circles lost power one day and clearly spiraled. He wound up creating a self-hosted backup internet, complete with photo syncing, messaging, streaming media, and offline Wikipedia. He was even kind enough to provide instructions for building your own 3D-printed doomsday briefcase. This content isn't visible due to your cookie preferences. ANDREW WEBSTERJUL 211 BitTorrent’s disastrous, legendary, and controversial story The file-sharing app launched 25 years ago and unleashed a wave of piracy that would shake Hollywood to its core. JANKO ROETTGERSJUL 263 Amazon has enough satellites to launch its Starlink competitor THOMAS RICKERJUL 212 JESS WEATHERBED JUL 2 Google’s $4.7 billion Android fine has been upheld. The search giant has lost its final appeal to overturn a record-breaking €4.1 billion (about $4.68 billion) penalty imposed by the European Commission over practices that abused Android’s mobile dominance. The charges date back to 2018, so it’s taken almost a decade to close the antitrust case. Google Android: the Court of Justice upholds Google’s fine of around €4.1 billion [COURT OF JUSTICE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION] 2 DOMINIC PRESTON JUL 2 Honor’s latest foldable is finally out in the UK and Europe. The Magic V6 is available to order now in the UK, Germany, France, Spain, and Italy. It’s officially priced at £1,999.99 / €2,299.99 (about $2,600), but Honor’s early offers slash hundreds off that price and throw in freebies like earbuds or tablets too. Honor’s Magic V6 sets three foldable firsts DOMINIC PRESTONJUN 15 6 Apple’s entry-level MacBook Pro could be up for a redesign JAY PETERSJUL 29 GABY DEL VALLE JUL 2 DHS won’t say whether it’s keeping a database of anti-ICE protesters. ICE has denied it maintains such a database, but Mother Jones lays out some shifty wording that suggests Trump administration officials are dodging the question — including a spontaneous mention of the known Terrorist Screening Dataset. Given that 15 Minnesota activists are facing federal charges, it sure sounds like DHS may be tracking protesters after all. ICE finds a new way to dodge Congress about a secret protester database [MOTHER JONES] 6 EMMA ROTH JUL 2 Apple’s email-hiding feature is reportedly exposing real email addresses. Tyler Murphy, the co-founder of EasyOptOuts, tells 404 Media that he notified Apple about a bug in its Hide My Email feature more than a year ago, but it’s reportedly still not fixed. The vulnerability allows someone to obtain a person’s real email through the fake one generated by Apple’s tool, 404 Media reports. Apple ‘Hide My Email’ Vulnerability Reveals Peoples’ Real Email Addresses [404 MEDIA] 11 Elon Musk denies a report about SpaceX’s AI phone prototype EMMA ROTHJUL 214 JAY PETERS JUL 2 Meta might launch its own cloud computing business. The company is weighing plans like selling access to AI models on its infrastructure or letting customers buy compute in a model similar to companies like CoreWeave, Bloomberg reports. Meta Is Planning a Cloud Business to Sell AI Computing Power [BLOOMBERG] 1 ANDREW WEBSTER JUL 2 It might finally be time to jack in. Apple is teasing its upcoming Neuromancer adaptation, which means we might be getting a trailer for the cyberpunk series — or maybe even a premiere date — in the near future. JENNIFER PATTISON TUOHYJUL 130 JESS WEATHERBED JUL 1 Former Sony gaming chief is baffled by the PlayStation PC pullback. In an interview with French media outlet PSI, former SIE Worldwide Studios chairman Shawn Layden said bringing Sony exclusives to PC was always about expanding title visability beyond PlayStation consoles, and wasn’t a money-making strategy: I think if someone’s waiting 18 months for something to come on PC, we didn’t lose a sale to them. They weren’t going to buy the hardware anyway. If it’s a way to cover costs or the burden of making a port, there is some money attached to that, or the distraction or whatever. I don’t know what they’re thinking.” 32 DOMINIC PRESTON JUL 1 Samsung starts teasing its wider foldable phone. All signs point to Samsung launching a wide version of the Galaxy Z Fold 8 this month, and now the company has released its first teaser. There are video versions too, all along similar lines teasing a “new shape” from the company. Image: Samsung 2 DOMINIC PRESTON JUL 1 The next bubble. If you loved Microsoft sticking Copilot in everything, just wait until its marketing department gets ahold of “quantum.“ itsmrfungible666: Get ready for Microsoft Windows 12 Elite with Copilot Quantum Get the day’s best comment and more in my free newsletter, The Verge Daily. 1 The cube was a lie: up close with our Dbrand Companion Cube before it gets destroyed SEAN HOLLISTERJUL 131 DOMINIC PRESTON JUL 1 Google AI Mode is making original recipe links more prominent. The AI search tool will start linking directly to source recipes at the top of cooking queries, with images, ratings, and ingredient numbers. Not every food writer is happy though, since there’s still a full AI-generated recipe below the links, sometimes with opaque sourcing and errors in the instructions. EU, an update. According to an unnamed spokesperson speaking to the FT, Tim Cook and EU tech chief Henna Virkkunen had a “constructive exchange on topics of common interest” on Tuesday over the current impasse that will leave roughly 450 million people without access to Apple Intelligence that actually works: The meeting included a discussion of how Apple can launch its reinvented Siri in Europe while avoiding millions of dollars in fines for violating the bloc’s flagship competition rules, according to two people familiar with the talks. Apple’s Tim Cook holds ‘constructive’ talks with EU tech chief over ‘Siri AI’ [FT] 16 RICHARD LAWLER JUL 1 Trump pulled in over $1 billion from crypto ventures last year. The President’s annual disclosure noted that companies owned by Trump and his family received more than $800 million from the WLFI token and $635 million in royalties from Trump memecoins, report Reuters, WSJ, and CNBC. CoinDesk points out the more than $50 million in Bitcoin, $25 million in ETH, investments in Coreweave and Coinbase, and $6 million from an NFT licensing agreement. Trump 2026 Annual OGE disclosure [DOCUMENTCLOUD] 9 Meta is adding ridiculous ‘rate limits’ and a soft paywall to its smart glasses SEAN HOLLISTERJUL 149 JAY PETERS JUL 1 Spark on Mac. Google is rolling out its frighteningly good AI agent to the Gemini macOS app, meaning Spark can access and work with files on your computer. The company is adding a few other new features to Spark, too, including the ability to connect Tasks and Keep to the agent, use integrations with apps like Canva and Instacart, and track topics in real time.
